---
title: "Empirical Finance from the Ground Up — ASSIP 2026 Cohort"
subtitle: "Eight full-time weeks of research-grade empirical finance, built for the GMU ASSIP 2026 group"
pagetitle: "Empirical Finance from the Ground Up — ASSIP 2026 Cohort"
description-meta: >
  An 8-week, full-time empirical finance research curriculum for the
  GMU ASSIP 2026 cohort. Hosted (remote) by Prof. Lei Gao at George
  Mason University's Costello College of Business. Probability, OLS,
  causal inference, quasi-experiments, text-as-data, and a research
  poster for the Aug 12, 2026 ASSIP Symposium.
toc: false
number-sections: false
page-layout: full
format:
  html:
    css: styles.scss
    include-in-header:
      - text: |
          <meta name="twitter:card" content="summary_large_image">
          <meta name="twitter:title" content="Empirical Finance from the Ground Up — ASSIP 2026 Cohort">
          <meta name="twitter:description" content="An 8-week full-time empirical-finance research curriculum for the GMU ASSIP 2026 cohort. Hosted remotely by Prof. Lei Gao at GMU.">
          <meta property="og:type" content="website">
          <meta property="og:title" content="Empirical Finance from the Ground Up — ASSIP 2026 Cohort">
          <meta property="og:description" content="An 8-week full-time empirical-finance research curriculum for the GMU ASSIP 2026 cohort. Hosted remotely by Prof. Lei Gao at GMU.">
          <meta property="og:site_name" content="Empirical Finance from the Ground Up — ASSIP 2026 Cohort">
---

::: {.hero}

::: {.hero-eyebrow}
George Mason University · College of Science · Aspiring Scientists Summer Internship Program (ASSIP) 2026
:::

# Empirical Finance from the Ground Up {.hero-title}

### ASSIP 2026 Cohort · Eight full-time weeks · A research poster at the symposium on Aug 12, 2026 {.hero-subtitle}

A textbook-and-lab system for the 14 advanced high-school and pre-graduation
undergraduate researchers in the **ASSIP 2026 Empirical Finance group** —
empirical finance the way it is actually done: with code, with data,
and with intellectual honesty about what we can and cannot conclude.

::: {.hero-cta}
[Start here →](book/00-front-matter/00-preface.md){.btn .btn-primary}
[How to use the book](book/00-front-matter/02-how-to-use.md){.btn .btn-outline}
[View source on GitHub](https://github.com/edwardlg/assip-2026-empirical-finance){.btn .btn-outline}
:::

::: {.hero-host}
Faculty mentor: **[Prof. Lei Gao](https://business.gmu.edu/)**, Associate Professor of Finance,
Costello College of Business, George Mason University. **2026 ASSIP slot: REMOTE only.**
Program director: Dr. Amanda Haymond Still · `cosassip@gmu.edu`.
:::

:::

::: {.quick-stats}
::: {.stat}
[**8**]{.stat-num} [Weeks]{.stat-lbl}
:::
::: {.stat}
[**40**]{.stat-num} [Chapters]{.stat-lbl}
:::
::: {.stat}
[**40**]{.stat-num} [Notebooks]{.stat-lbl}
:::
::: {.stat}
[**34**]{.stat-num} [Data cards]{.stat-lbl}
:::
::: {.stat}
[**5**]{.stat-num} [Capstones]{.stat-lbl}
:::
::: {.stat}
[**1**]{.stat-num} [Instructor manual]{.stat-lbl}
:::
:::

---

## Two interlinked sites

This project ships as **two sites** on a single GitHub Pages domain. Use the wiki for cohort logistics (calendars, mentors, templates, FAQ, symposium info); use the textbook for the chapters, problem sets, notebooks, capstones, and instructor's manual.

::: {.use-grid}
::: {.use-card}
### Cohort wiki — `/`
Operational dashboard: week-by-week schedule, project templates (PAP, poster), mentor roster, code of conduct, FAQ, symposium logistics, reading list, data access, setup guide.

[Open the wiki →](../){.btn .btn-outline}
:::

::: {.use-card}
### Textbook — `/textbook/` (you are here)
The 8-week curriculum: probability and inference, OLS, causal inference I & II, paper-reading guides, text/AI, two project weeks, plus appendices A–E, the capstone gallery, and the instructor's manual.

[Read the preface →](book/00-front-matter/00-preface.md){.btn .btn-outline}
:::
:::

---

## The 8-week arc — ASSIP 2026 calendar

Orientation is **Thursday Jun 18, 2026**. The cohort works **Mon–Fri 9 am – 5 pm**
from **Mon Jun 22** through the **ASSIP Research Symposium on Wed Aug 12**.
Holidays off: **Fri Jun 19** (Juneteenth) and **Fri Jul 3** (Independence Day observance).

::: {.week-grid}

::: {.week-card}
### [Week 1](book/weeks/week-01/README.md)
**Probability, Inference, and the Logic of Estimation**

The two laws, expectations as geometry, sampling distributions, LLN/CLT, the
mechanics of a $t$-test. Lab: simulating the *coin-flip universe*.
*Jun 22 – Jun 26, 2026.*
:::

::: {.week-card}
### [Week 2](book/weeks/week-02/README.md)
**OLS, Geometry, and Inference**

OLS in matrix form, Gauss-Markov, Frisch-Waugh-Lovell, robust and clustered
standard errors, omitted-variable bias. Lab: Fama-MacBeth on CRSP.
*Jun 29 – Jul 2, 2026 (Jul 3 off).*
:::

::: {.week-card}
### [Week 3](book/weeks/week-03/README.md)
**Causal Inference Foundations**

Potential outcomes and SUTVA, matching and propensity scores, entropy
balancing, IV and weak-IV pathology. Lab: making weak instruments lie.
*Jul 6 – Jul 10, 2026.*
:::

::: {.week-card}
### [Week 4](book/weeks/week-04/README.md)
**Quasi-Experiments in Finance**

Event studies, staggered DiD (TWFE vs. Callaway-Sant'Anna), regression
discontinuity, synthetic control, Bartik shift-share. Lab: HMDA DiD.
*Jul 13 – Jul 17, 2026.*
:::

::: {.week-card}
### [Week 5](book/weeks/week-05/README.md)
**Reading the Empirical Finance Canon**

Reader's guides to Fama-French 1992 & 1993, Jegadeesh-Titman 1993,
Petersen 2009, Bertrand-Duflo-Mullainathan 2004. *What does a JF paper actually do?*
*Jul 20 – Jul 24, 2026.*
:::

::: {.week-card}
### [Week 6](book/weeks/week-06/README.md)
**Text, AI, and Alternative Data**

Patent value, 10-K similarity, Loughran-McDonald sentiment, fair-lending
decomposition, and an AI copilot lab — without fooling yourself.
*Jul 27 – Jul 31, 2026.*
:::

::: {.week-card}
### [Week 7](book/weeks/week-07/README.md)
**Your Project, Part I**

Idea generation, data acquisition, pre-analysis plan, dataset
construction, identification memo. The week you stop being a student.
*Aug 3 – Aug 7, 2026.*
:::

::: {.week-card}
### [Week 8](book/weeks/week-08/README.md)
**Your Project, Part II — Symposium**

Specification curve, robustness battery, poster build, peer review,
defense. **Poster session at the ASSIP Research Symposium, Wed Aug 12.**
*Aug 10 – Aug 12, 2026.*
:::

:::

---

## Who this is for

This site is built for the **ASSIP 2026 Empirical Finance cohort** — a
group of **14 advanced high-school students (15+) and pre-graduation
undergraduates** selected by GMU's College of Science to work full-time,
Monday through Friday 9 am – 5 pm, from June 22 through the ASSIP
Research Symposium on August 12, 2026.

The group is at the high end of typical ASSIP group size (2–15), so we
run a **layered mentorship model**: Prof. Gao (faculty mentor, remote
slot) over the whole cohort, with PhD / RA sub-leaders working closely
with sub-pods on data engineering, identification, and writing. See
[Cohort & Mentorship Model](book/00-front-matter/04-cohort-and-mentorship.md)
for how that scales for 14 students.

The methodological bar is real. You should be comfortable with strong
precalculus or calculus, ready to learn Python quickly if you have not
yet, and willing to read math that begins:

$$
\hat{\boldsymbol{\beta}}_{\text{OLS}} = (\mathbf{X}'\mathbf{X})^{-1}\mathbf{X}'\mathbf{y}.
$$

If that equation is intimidating but interesting, you are exactly the
intended reader. Appendix A is a math toolkit; Appendix B is a setup
guide; the prerequisite self-test will tell you which gaps to close
before orientation on Jun 18.

::: {.cta-row}
[Take the prerequisite self-test →](book/00-front-matter/03-prerequisite-self-test.md){.btn .btn-outline}
[Cohort & mentorship model →](book/00-front-matter/04-cohort-and-mentorship.md){.btn .btn-outline}
:::

---

## What you'll build — and what ASSIP requires

The **required ASSIP 2026 deliverable** is a research poster presented
at the **ASSIP Research Symposium on Wed Aug 12, 2026** (in-person poster
session for on-site interns + virtual breakouts for remote interns —
which is all of us, since our 2026 slot is remote). Beyond that, by
the end of Week 8 you will have:

- A **research poster** for the Aug 12 symposium, audience includes Mason
  faculty, mentors, families, College of Science leadership, and Mason
  News press.
- A **research abstract** submitted to the *Journal of Student-Scientists'
  Research* (JSSR, GMU Mason Publishing).
- An optional **full paper** if your project supports it.
- A **public replication packet**: code, data references, README,
  environment file, one-command rebuild.
- **3 GMU College of Science credits** for completion, plus participation
  in the ASSIP scientific-writing and career forum.
- **Reading fluency** in five canonical empirical-finance papers and
  five more from the text/AI tradition.

---

## How to use the book

::: {.use-grid}
::: {.use-card}
### For students
Read each week's chapters in order. Work the problem sets *before*
opening the solutions. Run every notebook. The labs are the part where
you actually learn. Anchor every analysis decision to the
identification memo you will write in Week 7.
:::

::: {.use-card}
### For instructors & mentors
The [instructor manual](book/instructor-manual/README.md) has the
ASSIP-specific pacing guide, grading rubrics, common pitfalls, and
answer keys. The [capstone gallery](book/capstones/README.md) gives
five fully-specified projects with synthetic illustrative results.
:::

::: {.use-card}
### For independent readers
You can run every notebook on **Colab** or **Binder** directly from each
notebook page — look for the buttons at the top. The
[`environment.yml`](https://github.com/edwardlg/assip-2026-empirical-finance/blob/main/environment.yml)
pins the conda environment.
:::

::: {.use-card}
### For everyone
Every chapter that makes a quantitative claim flags the claim with a
`[CHECK]` tag if it has not yet been verified against a primary source.
This is on purpose. Research is a process, not a posture.
:::
:::

---

## Acknowledgements

This curriculum is built for the **ASSIP 2026 (Aspiring Scientists
Summer Internship Program)** at George Mason University's College of
Science — director **Dr. Amanda Haymond Still**, program contact
`cosassip@gmu.edu`. ASSIP makes research-grade methods accessible to
high-school students and pre-graduation undergraduates who are ready
for them, regardless of GPA, and offers full tuition waivers for
Pell-eligible and reduced-lunch students. The
[articulation matrix](book/00-front-matter/01-articulation-matrix.md)
maps every learning objective to the ASSIP symposium deliverable and to
downstream undergraduate / PhD-level competencies, with sources cited.

The textbook was drafted in a multi-agent workflow that pairs planning,
writing, and review agents under human supervision. See [About](about.qmd)
for the methodology and the editorial standard.

---

::: {.footer-cta}
Ready to start?

[Read the preface →](book/00-front-matter/00-preface.md){.btn .btn-primary}
[Browse the capstones →](book/capstones/README.md){.btn .btn-outline}
[Open on GitHub →](https://github.com/edwardlg/assip-2026-empirical-finance){.btn .btn-outline}
:::
